channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Who is Sean Curran? Head of Trump's personal detail to be nominated for Secret Service director
item.description: Sean Curran, the head of President-elect Trump's personal security detail, will be tapped to lead the U.S. Secret Service, according to the president's son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida hotel discriminated against Arab American group after Hamas' Oct. 7 attack against Israel, DOJ says
item.description: A Florida hotel owner reached an agreement with the DOJ, which accused the hotel of discrimination after it canceled a planned conference for an Arab American group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters from the Navajo Nation worked tirelessly through a haze of dust to cut away dirt from a narrow road at the side of a mountain struck by a landslide in Southern California, coughing and sneezing amid the backbreaking work.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LOCHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The rise - and potential fall - of TikTok in the US
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing app. Now, the moment its fans dreaded is here, but uncertainty over TikTok’s future lingers.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Resettlement agencies race to help refugees ahead of Trump's second term
item.description: NEW MILFORD, Conn. (AP) — Rogers Lopez knows just how lucky his family is as they settle into their furnished two-bedroom apartment in suburban Connecticut, just before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: South Carolina man pleads guilty to the carjacking and killing of a New Mexico police officer
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A South Carolina man pleaded guilty Friday to charges that he gunned down a New Mexico state police officer who had stopped to help him, marking a turning point in a case that had been set for trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bribery, fraud charges dropped against former New York Lt. Governor
item.description: Federal prosecutors have dropped bribery and fraud charges against former New York Lt. Gov. Brian Benjamin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Busta Rhymes turns himself in after fight with man in NYC, police say
item.description: Trevor Tanhiem Smith, Jr., 52, also known as Busta Rhymes, is accused of punching a man in the face during a fight last week, police said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: A battery plant fire in California started during a boom for energy storage
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A fire at one of the world’s largest battery plants in Northern California contained tens of thousands of lithium batteries that store power from renewable energy and have become a growing electricity source.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen MS-13 victim's father slams last-minute Biden DOJ plea deal
item.description: Robert Mickens says no murderers should be granted plea deals, but this one comes as a 'slap in the face' with a suspect who admitted to killing seven.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The baby names going 'extinct' in 2025, according to BabyCenter
item.description: Much like the doomed flightless dodo, baby names like “Emory,” “Jamie” or “Remy” are quickly being stamped out by the same humans who once found them so uniquely remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Homes were burning and roads already jammed when Pacific Palisades evacuation order came, AP finds
item.description: An Associated Press analysis of emergency communications shows that the first evacuation order covering neighborhoods closest to the start of the devastating Pacific Palisades wildfire didn’t come until about 40 minutes after some of those homes were already burning.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Gene Johnson, Christopher L. Keller, Claudia Lauer And Amy Taxin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation’s highest court upholding a law that’s set to officially cut the cord and halt new downloads off the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Wyatte Grantham-Philips And Mae Anderson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bail denied for American captured in France and accused of assaulting Pennsylvania student
item.description: EAST BERLIN, Pa. (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was ordered held without bail Friday during a court hearing in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida jury says CNN defamed Navy veteran in story about endangered Afghans
item.description: A jury in Panama City, Florida, on Friday found that CNN defamed a U.S. Navy veteran in a 2021 story about people paid to rescue endangered Afghans following the Taliban takeover of the country.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top NJ watchdog official abruptly resigns, is removed from state voter rolls following residency flap
item.description: Tiffany Williams Brewer reportedly claimed a Maryland property as her principal residence, but later voted in New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants one of the agents who covered him after he was shot to lead the Secret Service
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will choose to lead the U.S. Secret Service one of the agents who covered him after a gunmen opened fire at the candidate during a campaign rally in Pennsylvania. Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted expected to fill JD Vance's Senate seat
item.description: DeWine is expected to announce Friday that he is selecting Jon Husted to fill the Senate seat vacated by JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trump inauguration moved indoors because of freezing temperatures
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony is being moved indoors because of expected bitter cold Monday in Washington, D.C. Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance are expected to be sworn in inside the Capitol Rotunda rather than on the West Front of the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden affirms Equal Rights Amendment is part of Constitution
item.description: President Joe Biden on Friday asserted that the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA), which prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex, is part of the Constitution, arguing it had met the criteria to be added as the 28th amendment.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, KRON4

item.title: Cowboy Carter Chronicles: From Juneteenth to Sundown Towns, a new chapter in American History
item.description: Discover forgotten Black history in the American West with the #CowboyCarterChronicles! From Juneteenth to sundown towns, explore new pathways to freedom inspired by Beyoncé.
item.dc:creator: Jyesha Johnson, KPRC 2

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel and Hamas reach a ceasefire deal
item.description: President Joe Biden says Israel and Hamas have agreed to a ceasefire deal to pause the war in Gaza and release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, KRON4

item.title: Biden administration cancels loans for 260,000 former Ashford University students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 260,000 former students of the now-defunct for-profit Ashford University are getting their student loans erased as the Biden administration presses ahead with debt forgiveness in its final days.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
